title: SACRAMENTO –
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: Assembly Bill 669 reauthorizes the California Department of Transportation to test Cooperative Adaptive Cruise Control technologies.
CACC technologies allow trucks to safely follow each other with less than 100 feet between vehicles by syncing acceleration and braking.
Platooning enhances safety and lessens traffic.
CACC technologies can reduce fuel consumption by 10 to 15 percent.
Platooning could lead to 4.5% fuel savings for the lead truck and 10% fuel savings for the following truck.
The goods movement sector accounts for half the state’s diesel consumption.
Fuel savings from CACC technologies will have a significant, positive impact on air quality.
